snippet: The general purpose of the financial statements is to provide information about the results of operations, financial position, and cash flows of an organisation. This information is used to make decisions regarding the allocation of resources and provide governance over business operations. Undertake a bank reconciliation and prepare a report for each active bank account at the end of each month. Provide a written explanation of any differences on the bank reconciliation report. Have the bank reconciliation report certified by the principal. Ensure that bank accounts do not show a negative balance (are overdrawn) either at the bank or in CASES21.
